     - Added `InlineCitationGroup` component:
       ```tsx
       function InlineCitationGroup({ badges, activeCitation, onCiteClick }) {
         const [expanded, setExpanded] = useState(false)
         const collapsible = badges.length >= 3
         return (
           <span className="inline-flex items-center gap-0.5 align-middle">
             <CitationBadge citation={badges[0]} ... />
             {collapsible && (
               <button onClick={() => setExpanded(p => !p)} ...>
                 {expanded ? <ChevronsRightLeft className="size-3" /> : "В·В·В·"}
               </button>
             )}
             {(expanded || !collapsible) && badges.slice(1).map(c => <CitationBadge key={c.id} ... />)}
           </span>
         )
       }
       ```
     - Added `CitationBadgeRow` component with `VISIBLE_THRESHOLD = 2`
     - `AssistantContent` text pre-processing: groups consecutive `[N][M]` into `<cite data-indices="N,M">`:
       ```ts
       const processed = citations.length > 0
         ? text
             .replace(/(\[\d+\])(\s*\[\d+\])+/g, (match) => {
               const indices = [...match.matchAll(/\[(\d+)\]/g)].map(m => m[1])
               return `<cite data-indices="${indices.join(",")}"></cite>`
             })
             .replace(/\[(\d+)\]/g, '<cite data-idx="$1"></cite>')
         : text
       ```
     - `cite` component handler checks `data-indices` вҶ’ renders `InlineCitationGroup`
     - Import changed to `resolveS3ImagesInContent`
     - `CitationHoverContent`: `<Markdown>{resolveS3ImagesInContent(citation.content)}</Markdown>`
     - `AssistantContent`: `{resolveS3ImagesInContent(processed)}` passed to Markdown
     - `img` component handler: simplified (no more `resolveS3ImageSrc`)

   - **`app/api/sources/[sourceId]/preview/route.ts`**
     - Changed from returning first chunk to ALL chunks joined:
       ```ts
       const chunks = await db.select({...}).from(parentChunks)
         .where(eq(parentChunks.sourceId, sourceId))
         .orderBy(asc(parentChunks.positionStart), asc(parentChunks.createdAt))
       const content = chunks.map(c => c.content).join("\n\n")
       ```

   - **`lib/ingestion/pdf.ts`** вҖ” Complete rewrite with mupdf:
     ```ts
     export interface PdfPage {
       pageNumber: number
       content: string // Markdown with s3:// image refs
     }
     
     export async function parsePdf(s3Key: string, sourceId: string): Promise<PdfParseResult> {
       // Downloads from S3, opens with mupdf
       // For each page: walk() collecting text+image blocks by Y-position
       // Images вүҘ100Г—100px uploaded to S3 as PNG
       // Returns pages with interleaved Markdown content
     }
     ```
     Key walk callback pattern:
     ```ts
     st.walk({
       beginTextBlock(bbox) { curBlockY = bbox[1]; curLines = [] },
       endTextBlock() { blocks.push({ y: curBlockY, type: "text", text }) },
       beginLine() { curChars = [] },
       endLine() { curLines.push(curChars.join("").trim()) },
       onChar(char) { curChars.push(char) },
       onImageBlock(bbox, _transform, image) {
         if (image.getWidth() < 100 || image.getHeight() < 100) return
         // deduplicate by bbox key
         const png = image.toPixmap().asPNG()
         blocks.push({ y: bbox[1], type: "image", png, key: `sources/${sourceId}/images/page${i+1}-img${idx++}.png` })
       }
     })
     ```

   - **`lib/ingestion/chunking.ts`**
     - `PdfPage.text` вҶ’ `PdfPage.content`
     - New `stripImages(content)`: `content.replace(/!\[.*?\]\(s3:\/\/[^)]+\)/g, "")`
     - New `splitIntoSegments(content)`: handles image markdown as atomic units
     - `buildChildChunks()` calls `stripImages(parent.content)` before splitting

   - **`lib/ingestion/pipeline.ts`**
     - `parsePdf(s3Key)` вҶ’ `parsePdf(s3Key, sourceId)`

   - **`lib/ingestion/web.ts`** and **`lib/ingestion/youtube.ts`**
     - Changed `{ text: ..., pageNumber: ... }` to `{ content: ..., pageNumber: ... }` in PdfPage objects

   - **`app/api/s3-image/route.ts`** вҖ” New file:
     ```ts
     export async function GET(req: Request) {
       const session = await auth()
       if (!session?.user?.id) return new Response("Unauthorized", { status: 401 })
       const key = new URL(req.url).searchParams.get("key")
       if (!key) return new Response("Missing key", { status: 400 })
       if (!key.startsWith("sources/") || !key.includes("/images/"))
         return new Response("Forbidden", { status: 403 })
       const res = await s3.send(new GetObjectCommand({ Bucket: S3_BUCKET, Key: key }))
       const bytes = await res.Body!.transformToByteArray()
       return new Response(bytes.buffer as ArrayBuffer, {
         headers: { "Content-Type": res.ContentType ?? "image/png", "Cache-Control": "private, max-age=3600" }
       })
     }
     ```

   - **`lib/s3-image-url.ts`** вҖ” New file:
     ```ts
     export function resolveS3ImageSrc(src: string | undefined): string | undefined {
       if (!src) return undefined
       if (src.startsWith("s3://")) return `/api/s3-image?key=${encodeURIComponent(src.slice(5))}`
       return src
     }
     
     export function resolveS3ImagesInContent(content: string): string {
       return content.replace(
         /!\[([^\]]*)\]\(s3:\/\/([^)]+)\)/g,
         (_, alt, key) => `![${alt}](/api/s3-image?key=${encodeURIComponent(key)})`,
       )
     }
     ```

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **mupdf `toPixmap().asPNG()` returns Uint8Array вҖ” type mismatch with Response constructor**: Fixed by using `bytes.buffer as ArrayBuffer` in s3-image route
   - **`PdfPage.text` property renamed to `content`**: web.ts and youtube.ts still used `text` вҶ’ fixed with sed substitution
   - **`resolveS3ImageSrc` returned `""` for empty string src**: Fixed by changing `if (!src) return src` to `if (!src) return undefined`
   - **`s3://` protocol sanitized by micromark to empty string**: Root cause of images not displaying. `micromark-util-sanitize-uri` only allows `https?` for `img[src]`. The `img` React component handler receives `""` not `s3://...`. Fixed by pre-processing entire Markdown content string with `resolveS3ImagesInContent()` BEFORE passing to `<Markdown>`, converting `s3://key` вҶ’ `/api/s3-image?key=enckey` (relative URL passes sanitization)
   - **User exposed DATABASE_URL in command output**: Was rejected by user with "Du darfst nie variablen aus der .env exponieren!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!". Fixed by using `set -a && source .env && set +a && psql "$DATABASE_URL"` pattern
   - **`linter changed VISIBLE_THRESHOLD from 2 to 1`**: The linter/formatter changed the constant; confirmed file still shows `2` when read directly
   - **`ChevronsRightLeft` used but `ChevronLeft`/`ChevronRight` references remained**: Fixed by removing old references when switching to the single icon

5. Problem Solving:
   - **PDF image extraction**: pdfjs-dist only extracts text and can't decode JPEG 2000 (JPX) format images. Switched to mupdf.js which handles both text and image extraction natively, with `walk()` API providing Y-position coordinates for proper interleaving
   - **Image URL security**: `s3://` is a non-standard URL scheme rejected by react-markdown's parser. Solution: pre-process content string before Markdown rendering instead of relying on `img` component handler (which receives the already-sanitized empty string)
   - **Duplicate images in PDF**: Many PDFs reference the same image multiple times (masks, transparency groups). Fixed by deduplicating via Set of rounded bbox coordinate strings
   - **Citation source preview showed only first chunk**: Changed preview API to load ALL parent chunks and join them

## рҹӨ– Assistant

Gut. `sharp` Гјbernimmt. NГӨchste Frage:

**Frage 2: Welchen Schwellenwert fГјr die Laplacian Variance?**

`sharp` berechnet nach `.convolve()` mit dem Laplacian-Kernel die Pixelstatistiken per `.stats()`. Die Varianz ergibt sich aus `stdevВІ`. Typische Richtwerte:

| Varianz | SchГӨrfe |
|---------|---------|
| < 50 | deutlich unscharf (Scan-Artefakt, komprimiertes Thumbnail) |
| 50вҖ“200 | leicht unscharf |
| 200вҖ“1000 | normal bis scharf |
| > 1000 | sehr scharf (Diagramm, Text) |

Der richtige Wert hГӨngt stark vom Bildinhalt ab вҖ” ein weiГҹes Bild mit Text hat viel hГ¶here Varianz als ein Foto mit sanften ГңbergГӨngen.

**Empfehlung: 100 als hardcodierter Startwert** вҖ” konservativ genug, um echte Scan-Artefakte zu filtern, ohne versehentlich inhaltlich relevante Fotos zu verwerfen. Wir kГ¶nnen ihn in einer Konstante benennen (`MIN_BLUR_VARIANCE = 100`) damit er leicht anpassbar ist.

Alternativen:
- **50**: Filtert nur extremste FГӨlle (fast alles kommt durch)
- **200**: Aggressiver, filtert auch leicht unscharfe Fotos

Passt 100 als Startwert, oder willst du einen anderen?

`sharp` ist noch nicht installiert. Das ГӨndert die AbwГӨgung:

**Frage 4: `sharp` installieren oder pure JS direkt auf mupdf-Pixmap?**

mupdf's `Pixmap` hat `getSamples()` вҶ’ liefert rohe Pixelbytes als `Uint8Array`. Damit kГ¶nnen wir Laplacian Variance ohne neue Dependency berechnen:

```ts
const pixmap = image.toPixmap()
const samples = pixmap.getSamples()  // raw RGBA bytes
// вҶ’ Graustufen-Konvertierung + 3Г—3 Kernel pixelweise вҶ’ Varianz
```

**A (Empfehlung): Pure JS auf Pixmap-Samples** вҖ” kein neuer Dependency, kein nativer Build-Schritt, lГӨuft direkt auf dem was mupdf bereits liefert. ~30 Zeilen JS fГјr Graustufenkonvertierung + Laplacian-Kernel + Varianz. Kein `npm install`, kein potentieller Konflikt mit Next.js-Build.

**B: `sharp` installieren** вҖ” sauberer API-Aufruf (`.grayscale().convolve().stats()`), aber: neue native Dependency, `sharp` braucht `libvips` als System-Library, kann in manchen Deployment-Umgebungen Probleme machen.

Ohne zwingende GegengrГјnde fГјr `sharp` wГјrde ich bei **A** bleiben вҖ” wir haben die Pixeldaten bereits.

## рҹӨ– Assistant

Committed. Was gebaut wurde:

**Ingestion** (`pipeline.ts`): Ein LLM-Call gibt jetzt JSON mit `summary` + `topics[]` zurГјck. Topics sind 4вҖ“6 kurze deutsche Fragen, je max. 6 WГ¶rter. Fallback auf reinen Text wenn das JSON-Parsing fehlschlГӨgt.

**Badge-UI** (`source-sidebar.tsx`): Unterhalb des Summary-Texts erscheinen die Topic-Badges in `primary`-Farbe als Pill-Buttons вҖ” nur wenn die Zusammenfassung ausgeklappt ist.

**Chat-Bridge** (`chat-panel.tsx`): `window.CustomEvent("notebook:ask")` wird abgehГ¶rt. Klick auf einen Badge feuert das Event вҶ’ ChatPanel empfГӨngt es und sendet die Frage direkt via `sendMessage()`, ohne den Input-State zu berГјhren. Ignoriert Events wenn gerade gestreamt wird oder keine Quellen bereit sind.

Bestehende Quellen brauchen Re-Upload damit Topics generiert werden.

Zur Frage **DB-Reset vor jedem Test**: Ja, das ist mГ¶glich und wГӨre die sauberere LГ¶sung. Der Ansatz wГӨre:

**Option A: Truncate in `global-setup.ts`** вҖ” vor dem gesamten Test-Run alle relevanten Tabellen leeren (`TRUNCATE notebooks CASCADE`). Schnell, aber kein per-Test-Isolation.

**Option B: Transaktions-Rollback pro Test** вҖ” jeder Test lГӨuft in einer DB-Transaktion die am Ende zurГјckgerollt wird. Funktioniert gut fГјr Unit/Integration-Tests, aber **nicht fГјr Playwright E2E** вҖ” der Browser und der Next.js-Server laufen in separaten Prozessen, die DB-Verbindung des Servers ist nicht dieselbe wie die des Tests.

**Option C: `afterEach` mit API-Call / direktem DB-Cleanup** вҖ” nach jedem Test gezielt die erstellten Ressourcen lГ¶schen. Das ist im Wesentlichen was wir jetzt haben.

**Empfehlung**: FГјr diese E2E-Tests bleibt **Option C (afterEach)** die praktikabelste LГ¶sung вҖ” Playwright-Tests sind per Natur Integrationstest gegen einen laufenden Server. Der DB-Reset wГјrde nur helfen wenn Tests parallel laufen und sich gegenseitig stГ¶ren, was hier mit eindeutigen Namen kein Problem ist.

Soll ich trotzdem einen `global-setup` Truncate fГјr den Local-Test-Run einbauen?

---

  - `command`: git add tests/notebooks.spec.ts tests/sources.local.spec.ts tests/chat.local.spec.ts && git commit -m "test: guarantee n

- **`sources.local.spec.ts`**: `notebookName` wird am Testanfang gesetzt, `afterEach` rГӨumt auf

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   This session worked on multiple features for Issue #8 (RAG Chat) and testing:
   - **QuellenГјbersicht**: AI-generated source summary as collapsible violet card dropdown at top of source detail panel, with Markdown rendering and bold keywords
   - **Topic Badges**: 4-6 suggested German question badges per source generated by LLM, displayed below summary, clicking sends question to chat via `CustomEvent`
   - **E2E Tests**: `chat.local.spec.ts` testing RAG chat flow, citation badges, source detail, topic badges
   - **afterEach cleanup**: Guarantee notebook deletion in all test files even on failure
   - **Test debugging**: Multiple rounds of fixing test failures

   **Security constraints (must remain in effect):**
   - NEVER print `.env` values вҖ” only check key existence: `grep -q "KEY" .env && echo set || echo missing`
   - NEVER edit `.env` files directly вҖ” only point out mismatches to the user
   - User stated verbatim: "Du darfst nie variablen aus der .env exponieren!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!"

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **generateSourceMeta()**: Single Claude LLM call returning `{ summary: string, topics: string[] }` as JSON; non-fatal (`.catch()` returns null if API fails)
   - **CustomEvent bridge**: `window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("notebook:ask", { detail: { text } }))` fired from sidebar; ChatPanel listens via `useEffect` and calls `sendMessage({ text })`
   - **activeUpload.title**: Source sidebar shows source filename IMMEDIATELY during processing inside `<aside>` вҖ” `getByText("kreta")` matches before source is ready; true readiness signal is `getByPlaceholder("Text eingebenвҖҰ")`  
   - **ANTHROPIC_API_KEY missing from .env.test**: Causes `generateSourceMeta` to throw вҶ’ catch block deletes source вҶ’ page never reloads вҶ’ test waits forever
   - **MSW mock for Anthropic API**: Non-streaming вҶ’ JSON summary response; streaming вҶ’ SSE with `[1]` citation
   - **expect.timeout**: User changed from 10 min to 30 seconds in `playwright.test.config.ts`

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`db/schema.ts`**:
     - Added `summary: text("summary")` and `suggestedTopics: jsonb("suggested_topics").$type<string[]>()` to `sources` table

   - **`lib/ingestion/pipeline.ts`**:
     - Added `generateSourceMeta(pages): Promise<{ summary, topics }>` вҖ” one LLM call returning JSON
     - All three ingest flows (PDF, URL, YouTube) call it with `.catch(() => ({ summary: null, topics: [] }))` вҖ” non-fatal
     ```ts
     const meta = await generateSourceMeta(pages).catch((err) => {
       console.warn(`[ingest] Summary generation failed for ${sourceId}:`, err)
       return { summary: null, topics: [] }
     })
     await db.update(sources).set({ status: "ready", summary: meta.summary, suggestedTopics: meta.topics }).where(eq(sources.id, sourceId))
     ```
     - Prompt: `"Analyze the following document and respond with a JSON object (no markdown fences) with two keys: summary (3-5 German sentences with **bold** key terms) and topics (4-6 short German questions, max 6 words each)"`

   - **`lib/types/chat.ts`**:
     ```ts
     export type CitationChunk = {
       index: number; id: string; sourceTitle: string; sourceType: string
       sourceSummary?: string | null
       sourceTopics?: string[] | null
       url: string | null; positionStart: number | null; pageNumber: number | null; content: string
     }
     ```

   - **`app/api/sources/[sourceId]/preview/route.ts`**:
     - SELECT includes `summary: sources.summary, suggestedTopics: sources.suggestedTopics`
     - Response includes `sourceSummary: source.summary, sourceTopics: source.suggestedTopics`

   - **`app/api/chat/route.ts`**:
     - SELECT includes `sourceSummary: sources.summary, sourceTopics: sources.suggestedTopics`
     - CitationChunk mapping includes `sourceSummary: c.sourceSummary, sourceTopics: c.sourceTopics as string[] | null`

   - **`components/notebook/source-sidebar.tsx`**:
     - Imports: `ChevronDown, ChevronUp, Sparkles` added
     - `SourceDetailPanel` has `const [summaryOpen, setSummaryOpen] = useState(false)`
     - QuellenГјbersicht card (shrink-0, above scrollable content):
     ```tsx
     {chunk.sourceSummary && (
       <div className="shrink-0 px-3 py-2">
         <div className="overflow-hidden rounded-lg bg-violet-500/10 ring-1 ring-violet-500/20">
           <button onClick={() => setSummaryOpen(p => !p)} className="flex w-full items-center gap-2 px-3 py-2.5 text-left text-sm hover:bg-violet-500/10">
             <Sparkles className="size-3.5 shrink-0 text-violet-400" />
             <span className="flex-1 font-medium text-violet-100">QuellenГјbersicht</span>
             {summaryOpen ? <ChevronUp .../> : <ChevronDown .../>}
           </button>
           {summaryOpen && (
             <div className="max-h-64 overflow-y-auto border-t border-violet-500/20 px-3 py-3 text-sm leading-relaxed text-foreground">
               <Markdown remarkPlugins={[remarkGfm]}>{chunk.sourceSummary}</Markdown>
               {chunk.sourceTopics?.length > 0 && (
                 <div className="mt-3 flex flex-wrap gap-1.5">
                   {chunk.sourceTopics.map(topic => (
                     <button key={topic}
                       onClick={() => window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("notebook:ask", { detail: { text: topic } }))}
                       className="rounded-full border border-violet-500/30 bg-violet-500/15 px-2.5 py-0.5 text-xs text-violet-300 hover:bg-violet-500/25">
                       {topic}
                     </button>
                   ))}
                 </div>
               )}
             </div>
           )}
         </div>
       </div>
     )}
     ```

   - **`components/notebook/chat-panel.tsx`**:
     - Added `useEffect` for `notebook:ask` CustomEvent:
     ```ts
     useEffect(() => {
       function onAsk(e: Event) {
         const text = (e as CustomEvent<{ text: string }>).detail?.text?.trim()
         if (!text || isStreaming || readySourceCount === 0) return
         setInput("")
         setActiveCitation(null)
         sendMessage({ text })
       }
       window.addEventListener("notebook:ask", onAsk)
       return () => window.removeEventListener("notebook:ask", onAsk)
     }, [isStreaming, readySourceCount, sendMessage])
     ```

   - **`tests/chat.local.spec.ts`** (current state after user edits):
     ```ts
     async function ingestPdf(page: Page, pdfPath: string) {
       await page.getByRole("button", { name: "Quellen hinzufГјgen" }).click()
       const [fileChooser] = await Promise.all([
         page.waitForEvent("filechooser"),
         page.getByRole("button", { name: "Dateien hochladen" }).click(),
       ])
       await fileChooser.setFiles(pdfPath)
       await page.getByRole("dialog").waitFor({ state: "detached" })
     }
     // Tests use: page.getByPlaceholder("Text eingeben...")  вҶҗ three dots (user changed this)
     ```
     - 4 tests with `afterEach` cleanup via `tryDeleteNotebook`

   - **`tests/mocks/handlers.ts`**:
     - Added Anthropic API mock:
     ```ts
     http.post("https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages", async ({ request }) => {
       const body = await request.json()
       if (!body.stream) {
         return HttpResponse.json({ content: [{ type: "text", text: JSON.stringify({ summary: "...", topics: [...] }) }], ... })
       }
       // SSE streaming with "[1]" citation in text
       const stream = new ReadableStream({ start(controller) { /* SSE events */ } })
       return new HttpResponse(stream, { headers: { "Content-Type": "text/event-stream" } })
     })
     ```

   - **`playwright.test.config.ts`** (user-modified):
     - `expect.timeout` changed from `10 * 60 * 1000` to `30 * 1000`

   - **`tests/notebooks.spec.ts`** and **`tests/sources.local.spec.ts`**:
     - Added `tryDeleteNotebook()` helper and `test.afterEach` cleanup pattern

   - **Issue #19**: Created GitHub issue for blur detection of PDF images via Laplacian Variance (nice-to-have)

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Modal still open when filling chat input**: Added wait for modal to auto-close. User correction: don't click close button, wait passively for page reload.
   - **`networkidle` rejected by user**: "niemals networkidle da wir evtl ein polling problem dann haben" вҶ’ changed to `toBeEnabled()`

   - **Dialog never detaches (10 min timeout)**: Root cause: `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` missing from `.env.test` вҶ’ `generateSourceMeta` throws вҶ’ source deleted вҶ’ page never reloads. Two fixes: (1) non-fatal `.catch()` on `generateSourceMeta`, (2) MSW mock for Anthropic API

5. Problem Solving:
   - The core E2E test challenge: sources show in sidebar immediately during processing, making it hard to detect actual readiness
   - True readiness signal: `getByPlaceholder("Text eingebenвҖҰ")` (only appears after page reload when `readySourceCount > 0`)
   - Test env has no `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` вҶ’ MSW mock covers both summary generation (non-streaming) and chat (streaming SSE with citations)

   **Issue #9 - Notes Feature (in progress):**
   - Notes section in StudioSidebar (below Studio tools with separator)
   - Note cards: icon + truncated text + 3-dot dropdown (LГ¶schen implemented, rest devTodo())
   - Clicking note вҶ’ "Notizen-Ansicht" (Studio tools disappear, editor takes over)
   - "Als Notiz speichern" button on each assistant chat message
   - Server actions for CRUD

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Playwright E2E tests with MSW interceptors for Voyage AI and Anthropic APIs
   - Deterministic fake embeddings as fallback for unknown content (avoids fixture sync problems)
   - Next.js production build bakes fixtures at build time (need to kill port 3001 + rebuild for changes to take effect)
   - `useTranslations("namespace")` from next-intl for all UI text
   - CustomEvent bus: `notebook:source-count-change` for sidebarвҶ’ChatPanel reactive count
   - Optimistic UI with revert on error for source toggle
   - Server Actions (auth-gated) for notes CRUD
   - `sourceMessageId` FK to `messages.id` with validation before insert (to avoid FK violation with AI SDK generated IDs)
   - `devTodo()` for placeholder buttons (per project convention)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`tests/mocks/handlers.ts`**:
     - Added `deterministicEmbedding()` fallback (1024-dim vector from content hash) instead of throwing when chunk not in fixture
     - Added Anthropic MSW mock (non-streaming вҶ’ JSON summary, streaming вҶ’ SSE with `[1]` citation)
     ```ts
     function deterministicEmbedding(content: string): number[] {
       const DIMS = 1024
       const emb = new Array<number>(DIMS).fill(0)
       for (let i = 0; i < content.length; i++) {
         emb[i % DIMS] += content.charCodeAt(i)
       }
       const norm = Math.sqrt(emb.reduce((s, v) => s + v * v, 0)) || 1
       return emb.map((v) => v / norm)
     }
     function lookupEmbedding(content: string): number[] {
       const fixture = allFixtures.find((f) => f.content === content)
       return fixture ? fixture.embedding : deterministicEmbedding(content)
     }
     ```

   - **`tests/chat.local.spec.ts`**:
     - `ingestPdf`: `getByPlaceholder("Text eingeben", { exact: false })` with `{ timeout: 5 * 60_000 }`
     - Each test calls `tryDeleteNotebook(page, notebookName)` BEFORE `createNotebook` for cleanup
     - Topic badge test scoped to `.bg-primary`: `page.locator(".bg-primary").filter({ hasText: topicText! })`
     - 4 tests with `afterEach` cleanup
     
   - **`components/notebook/chat-panel.tsx`**:
     - Changed placeholder to `"Text eingeben..."` / `"Keine Quellen verfГјgbar..."` (ASCII dots)
     - Added `activeSourceCount` state initialized from `readySourceCount` prop
     - Listens for `notebook:source-count-change` CustomEvent to update count reactively
     - All internal refs use `activeSourceCount` instead of `readySourceCount`
     
   - **`lib/actions/sources.ts`**:
     - Added `toggleSourceEnabled(sourceId, notebookId, enabled)` server action
     
   - **`components/notebook/source-sidebar.tsx`**:
     - Added `useTranslations("sources")` (next-intl)
     - Added `enabledMap` state, `handleToggleEnabled`, `handleToggleAll`, `emitSourceCount`
     - Checkbox placed AFTER DropdownMenu (ganz rechts in source row)
     - "Alle auswГӨhlen" row: `justify-end gap-2`, label directly left of checkbox
     - `emitSourceCount` dispatches `notebook:source-count-change` CustomEvent
     - QuellenГјbersicht summary body: `p-5` (equal padding all sides)
     
   - **`app/[locale]/app/[notebookId]/page.tsx`**:
     - `readySources` query: added `eq(sources.enabled, true)` filter

   - **`messages/de.json`** and **`messages/en.json`**:
     - Added `sources.selectAll`, `sources.enableSource`, `sources.disableSource`, `sources.toggleError`
     - Added full `notes` namespace: title, newNote, saveAsNote, placeholder, save, back, fromChat, noNotes, createSuccess, saveSuccess, deleteSuccess, deleteNote, setAsSource, setAllAsSource, exportToDocs, exportToSheets

   - **`lib/actions/notes.ts`** (NEW):
     ```ts
     "use server"
     export async function createNote(notebookId: string, content: string, sourceMessageId?: string)
     export async function updateNote(noteId: string, notebookId: string, content: string)
     export async function deleteNote(noteId: string, notebookId: string)
     ```
     - `createNote` validates sourceMessageId against DB before inserting (avoids FK violation)
     - Auth-gated via `requireUser()`, ownership verified via `requireNoteOwner()`
     
   - **`app/[locale]/app/[notebookId]/layout.tsx`**:
     - Added `notes` import from schema
     - Added parallel query: `db.select().from(notes).where(eq(notes.notebookId, notebookId)).orderBy(desc(notes.createdAt))`
     - Passes `notebookId={notebookId}` and `initialNotes={initialNotes}` to `StudioSidebar`

   - **`scripts/export-embeddings.ts`** (NEW):
     - Script to re-export chunk embeddings from DB into fixture files
     - Usage: `npx tsx scripts/export-embeddings.ts kreta tests/fixtures/kreta-embeddings.json`

   - **`playwright.test.config.ts`**:
     - `expect.timeout` is 30 seconds (user changed from 10 min); per-assertion timeout for ingestion wait is 5 min

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **MSW fixture sync error** (`No fixture embedding found for content "www.sunnycars.de..."`):
     - Root cause: test server (production build) had OLD fixture baked in; updating JSON doesn't take effect until rebuild
     - Fix: switched from hard error to deterministic fake embedding fallback; also re-exported 48 embeddings from dev DB
     - User correction: "du musst die .env.test sourcen" (use .env.test DB)
     
   - **Placeholder mismatch**:
     - Component had `"Text eingebenвҖҰ"` (U+2026 ellipsis), test used `"Text eingeben..."` (ASCII dots)
     - Fix: changed component to use ASCII dots to match test
     
   - **Topic badge assertion finding two elements**:
     - `page.getByText(topicText!)` matched both sidebar badge AND chat message
     - Fix: `page.locator(".bg-primary").filter({ hasText: topicText! })` scopes to user message bubble only
     
   - **Stale notebook data between test runs**:
     - Failed tests left notebooks in DB; next run's assertions found stale data
     - Fix: `tryDeleteNotebook(page, notebookName)` at start of each test before `createNotebook`
     
   - **Inline text in components**:
     - User corrected: "Bitte schreibe niemals text content inline. Wir sind multiligual und verwenden i18n dafГјr. merk dir das"
     - Fix: replaced all hardcoded strings from Issue #15 with `t("key")` via `useTranslations("sources")`
     - Memory saved as `feedback_no_inline_text.md`

5. Problem Solving:
   - Deterministic fallback embedding eliminates fixture drift problem permanently вҖ” any unknown content gets a consistent hash-based embedding without failing
   - `emitSourceCount` ensures ChatPanel shows correct active source count even without page reload
   - sourceMessageId validation in createNote avoids FK constraint violations when AI SDK message IDs don't match DB UUIDs

**Frage 3: Soll es eine maximale TextlГӨnge geben?**

Sehr lange Texte treiben Embedding-Kosten (Voyage AI) in die HГ¶he. PDF-Quellen haben bereits ein 50 MB-Limit.

Meine Empfehlung: **50.000 Zeichen** вҖ” reicht fГјr ~30 Seiten Text, Гјberschaubare Embedding-Kosten, und zeige einen ZeichenzГӨhler unten rechts in der Textarea (z.B. вҖһ12.450 / 50.000").

Wie soll der Export aussehen вҖ” neues Dokument erstellen, oder Download? Brauchen wir OAuth-Scopes fГјr Google?

## рҹӨ– Assistant

Google Sign-in ist schon eingebaut, aber nur mit `email`/`profile`-Scopes. Google Docs braucht `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.file`. Das hat Auswirkungen auf den Login-Flow.

**Frage 2: Wie sollen wir den zusГӨtzlichen Drive-Scope handhaben?**

Meine Empfehlung: **Separater "Mit Google verbinden"-Button** der nur bei Bedarf (beim ersten Klick auf "In Docs exportieren") ausgelГ¶st wird вҖ” als inkrementeller OAuth-Flow mit dem Drive-Scope. GitHub-User wГјrden eine entsprechende Meldung sehen ("Google-Konto verbinden um Docs zu exportieren").

Alternativen:
- Drive-Scope direkt beim normalen Google-Login anfordern (alle User sehen die Docs-Permission, auch wenn sie es nie nutzen)
- Kein OAuth вҖ” stattdessen einfach `.docx` oder `.md` downloaden

---

## рҹ§‘ User

KГ¶nnen wir nicht einfach beim klick auf den button den drive-scope anfragen ohne einen extra "Mit Google verbinden"-Button hinzuzufГјgen??

---

## рҹӨ– Assistant

Ja, das geht вҖ” mit **Google Identity Services (GIS)** im Browser. Beim Klick wird ein Google-Popup geГ¶ffnet (kein Seitenumbruch, kein Redirect), der kurzlebige Drive-Token wird sofort zum Erstellen des Docs verwendet und danach verworfen. Kein Backend-Umbau nГ¶tig.

**Frage 3: Soll der OAuth-Schritt als Popup erscheinen (GIS Token Model вҖ” kein Seitenwechsel) oder als voller Redirect?**

Meine Empfehlung: **Popup** вҖ” der User verlГӨsst YannBookLM nicht, nach dem Google-Login Г¶ffnet das neu erstellte Doc direkt in einem neuen Tab.

---

## рҹӨ– Assistant

Fertig. Der Flow:

1. Klick auf "In Docs exportieren" вҶ’ GIS-Script wird dynamisch geladen (einmalig)
2. Google-Popup erscheint mit `drive.file`-Scope-Anfrage
3. Markdown wird via `marked` zu HTML konvertiert
4. Multipart-Upload an Drive API вҶ’ Google Docs-Dokument wird erstellt
5. Neuer Tab Г¶ffnet direkt das fertige Dokument

**Voraussetzung:** `NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<dein Google Client ID>` in `.env` eintragen (gleicher Wert wie `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`). AuГҹerdem muss die Google Drive API im Cloud Console-Projekt aktiviert sein.

---

## рҹӨ– Assistant

Nein вҖ” die Google Drive API ist kostenlos. Es gibt ein kostenloses Kontingent von 1 Milliarde Anfragen pro Tag, was fГјr YannBookLM bei weitem ausreicht. Kosten entstehen erst wenn du das weit Гјberschreitest, was bei einem persГ¶nlichen Tool unrealistisch ist.

Einzige Voraussetzung: die API im Google Cloud Console-Projekt aktivieren (kostenlos, nur ein Klick).

ARGUMENTS: Feature: "Alle Notizen als Quelle festlegen". Kontext: YannBookLM, Dropdown-MenГј einer Notiz in der Ergebnisliste. Bestehend: "Als Quelle festlegen" erstellt eine text-Quelle aus Titel+Content der einzelnen Notiz, mit Hash-Duplikat-Check. Offene Fragen: Was genau passiert bei "Alle Notizen"? Werden alle Notizen des Notebooks als separate Quellen angelegt, oder werden sie zu einer einzigen Quelle zusammengefГјhrt? Was passiert mit Notizen die bereits als Quelle vorhanden sind (Hash-Check)?

---

## рҹӨ– Assistant

**Frage 1: Werden alle Notizen als separate Quellen angelegt, oder zu einer einzigen zusammengefГјhrt?**

Meine Empfehlung: **Separate Quellen** вҖ” jede Notiz bekommt ihren eigenen Eintrag in der `sources`-Tabelle, analog zu "Als Quelle festlegen" fГјr eine einzelne Notiz. So bleibt jede Notiz im RAG einzeln referenzierbar.

---

## рҹӨ– Assistant

Alle Notizen вҶ’ eine einzige text-Quelle, Format:

```
Titel 1

Content 1

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Titel 2

Content 2
```

**Frage 2: Wie soll der Titel der zusammengefГјhrten Quelle lauten?**

Meine Empfehlung: **"Notizen"** вҖ” kurz, eindeutig, passt als Quellenname in der Sidebar.

## рҹӨ– Assistant

Klar. Ich habe genug zum Implementieren. Zusammenfassung:

- Eine Quelle aus allen Notizen, Format: `{title}\n\n{content}` getrennt durch `\n\n---...---\n\n`
- Quelltitel: `"Alle Notizen " + new Date().toLocaleDateString(locale)`
- Hash-Duplikat-Check wie gehabt

## рҹ§‘ User

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   This session implemented multiple features for Issue #9 (Notes Feature) in YannBookLM (NotebookLM clone):
   - StudioSidebar with notes list + detail view
   - "Als Notiz speichern" button on assistant chat messages
   - Unified output list (notes + studio outputs) with relative timestamps
   - Section label changes ("Ergebnisse") + FAB button
   - "Kopierten Text einfГјgen" view in Add Source modal with 10k char limit + processing view
   - Blue StickyNote icon for text sources in left sidebar
   - TipTap rich text editor for notes with full toolbar (markdown export)
   - `NotebookShell` client wrapper that hides chat and expands StudioSidebar in note mode
   - Note title field with default "Neue Notiz" + auto-save debounce (1s) + no save button
   - "Als Quelle festlegen" вҖ” creates text source from note with SHA-256 hash-based duplicate prevention

   **Latest request (pending):** "wenn der texteditor von den notizen geГ¶ffnet ist soll er sich den platz mit dem chat 50:50 aufteilen. er soll nicht den chat verdecken oder verschwinden lassen." вҖ” The note editor and chat should share space 50:50, not hide the chat.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - TipTap v3 editor with `tiptap-markdown` for markdown serialization
   - `tiptap-markdown` bundles its own `link` mark (StarterKit's explicit Link causes duplicate warning)
   - `NotebookShell` client component managing `noteMode` state between `<main>` and `StudioSidebar`
   - Debounced auto-save (1s) via `useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>`
   - SHA-256 hash via `crypto.subtle.digest` for text source deduplication
   - `after()` from Next.js for background ingestion after API response
   - CustomEvents: `notebook:save-as-note` (ChatPanel вҶ’ StudioSidebar), `notebook:source-count-change`
   - Drizzle ORM schema migration: `drizzle-kit push` for adding `title` column to notes
   - `ingestText()` pipeline function вҖ” wraps text as single PdfPage, reuses chunking + embedding

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`components/notebook/studio-sidebar.tsx`** (major rewrite):
     - Props: `notebookId`, `initialNotes`, `initialStudioOutputs`, `noteMode`, `onNoteModeChange`
     - State: `collapsed`, `notesList`, `activeNote`, `editTitle`, `editContent`, `isSettingSource`, `debounceRef`
     - `OutputItem` unified type: `{ id, kind: "note" | StudioOutput["type"], title, createdAt, note? }`
     - `outputIcon(kind)` вҖ” StickyNote (blue-400) for notes, FA icons for others
     - `relativeTime(date)` helper
     - `scheduleSave(title, content, noteId)` вҖ” 1s debounce вҶ’ `updateNote()`
     - `handleSetAsSource()` вҖ” SHA-256 hash, check duplicate, POST `/api/sources`, reload
     - Note detail view: title `<input>` + `NoteEditor` + footer with "Als Quelle festlegen" button
     - `<aside>` width: `collapsed ? "w-12" : noteMode ? "flex-1" : "w-96"`
     - FAB at bottom: `absolute bottom-4 left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2`, pill shape

   - **`components/notebook/notebook-shell.tsx`** (NEW):
     ```tsx
     "use client"
     export function NotebookShell({ children, notebookId, initialNotes, initialStudioOutputs }) {
       const [noteMode, setNoteMode] = useState(false)
       return (
         <>
           <main className={cn("flex overflow-hidden transition-all duration-200", noteMode ? "w-0" : "flex-1")}>
             {children}
           </main>
           <StudioSidebar ... noteMode={noteMode} onNoteModeChange={setNoteMode} />
         </>
       )
     }
     ```
     **PENDING CHANGE**: `noteMode ? "w-0"` must become `flex-1` so chat stays visible at 50%.

   - **`components/notebook/note-editor.tsx`** (NEW):
     - TipTap editor with StarterKit, Placeholder, Markdown extensions
     - Toolbar: Undo/Redo, heading dropdown (Normal/H1/H2/H3), Bold, Italic, Link (via `window.prompt`), Code, CodeBlock, BulletList, OrderedList, Blockquote, HorizontalRule, ClearFormatting
     - `onChange(markdown)` via `(editor.storage as unknown as { markdown: { getMarkdown: () => string } }).markdown.getMarkdown()`
     - Initial content loaded via `editor.commands.setContent(initialContent)` in useEffect

   - **`components/notebook/chat-panel.tsx`**:
     - Added `SaveAsNoteButton` component (BookmarkPlus icon, hover-visible)
     - Fires `NOTE_FROM_CHAT_EVENT` CustomEvent with `{ content: clean, sourceMessageId: messageId }`
     - Strips `[N]` citation markers before saving

   - **`components/sources/add-source-modal.tsx`**:
     - Added `"text-input"` and `"text-processing"` to `View` type
     - `TEXT_MAX_CHARS = 10_000` constant
     - `handleTextSubmit`: derives title from first line (max 60 chars), checks limit, POSTs, polls, reloads
     - Char counter below textarea with red error state when exceeded
     - "Kopierter Text" button enabled (was `disabled` + `devTodo`)

   - **`app/api/sources/route.ts`**:
     - Added `TextPayload: { notebookId, type: "text", title, text, fileHash? }`
     - Handles `type: "text"`: validates empty/too-long, inserts with `fileHash`, calls `ingestText` via `after()`

   - **`lib/ingestion/pipeline.ts`**:
     - Added `ingestText(sourceId: string, text: string)`:
       ```ts
       const pages: PdfPage[] = [{ pageNumber: 1, content: text }]
       await ingestPages(sourceId, pages)
       // + generateSourceMeta + status updates
       ```

   - **`lib/actions/notes.ts`**:
     - `createNote(notebookId, title, content, sourceMessageId?)` вҖ” validates FK, inserts with title
     - `updateNote(noteId, notebookId, title, content)` вҖ” updates both title and content

   - **`db/schema.ts`**:
     - Added `title: text("title").notNull().default("Neue Notiz")` to notes table

   - **`drizzle/0000_rainy_archangel.sql`** (NEW):
     - Migration with `"title" text DEFAULT 'Neue Notiz' NOT NULL`

   - **`app/[locale]/app/[notebookId]/layout.tsx`**:
     - Replaced `<main>` + `<StudioSidebar>` with `<NotebookShell>`
     - Fetches `initialStudioOutputs` via parallel DB query
     - Passes `notebookId`, `initialNotes`, `initialStudioOutputs` to NotebookShell

   - **`components/notebook/source-sidebar.tsx`**:
     - Added `StickyNote` import from lucide-react
     - `SourceTypeIcon`: `if (type === "text") return <StickyNote className="size-4 shrink-0 text-blue-400" />`

   - **`messages/de.json`** and **`messages/en.json`**:
     - Added `studio.*`: title, audio, slidedeck, mindmap, datatable, quiz
     - Added `notes.*`: defaultTitle, outputsTitle, noOutputs, addNote, setAsSourceSuccess, setAsSourceDuplicate, setAsSourceError
     - Added `sources.*`: pastedTextTitle, pastedTextDescription, pastedTextPlaceholder, pastedTextTooLong, insert

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **Duplicate TipTap `link` extension warning**: `tiptap-markdown` registers its own `link` mark; removed explicit `@tiptap/extension-link` import and `Link.configure()` from extensions array
   - **TS error `Property 'markdown' does not exist on type 'Storage'`**: Cast `editor.storage as unknown as { markdown: { getMarkdown: () => string } }`
   - **`StudioSidebar is not defined` error**: Stale Next.js dev server cache; resolved naturally after layout.tsx was properly updated to use `NotebookShell`
   - **JSON parse error in de.json**: `вҖһ{title}"` вҖ” closing `"` (U+201C) was interpreted as JSON string terminator; fixed by removing title interpolation вҶ’ `"setAsSourceDuplicate": "Dieser Text ist bereits als Quelle vorhanden"`
   - **Missing `title` column in DB**: Added to schema + ran `drizzle-kit push` with `.env` sourced

5. Problem Solving:
   - Unified output list: `OutputItem` type merges notes and studio outputs, sorted by `createdAt` desc
   - 50:50 layout (pending): `NotebookShell` currently hides chat with `w-0` when `noteMode` is true; needs to become `flex-1` so both panels coexist
   - Auto-save debounce: single `debounceRef` timeout shared between title and content changes; cancelled on note close/delete
   - Hash-based deduplication: uses existing `/api/sources/check?hash=` endpoint with `fileHash` stored on text sources

ARGUMENTS: Feature: "Nach Google Sheets exportieren" fГјr Notizen. Kontext: YannBookLM, Dropdown einer Notiz in der Ergebnisliste. Eine Notiz hat title (plain text) + content (Markdown). Offene Fragen: Was soll in Sheets exportiert werden вҖ” nur diese eine Notiz, oder alle Notizen des Notebooks? Welches Format вҖ” eine Zeile pro Notiz (title | content), oder eine Zeile pro Absatz/Abschnitt? Brauchen wir einen neuen OAuth-Scope (spreadsheets)?

---

## рҹӨ– Assistant

**Frage 1: Wird nur diese eine Notiz exportiert, oder alle Notizen des Notebooks?**

Meine Empfehlung: **Alle Notizen** вҖ” ein Sheet mit allen Notizen ist deutlich nГјtzlicher als ein Sheet mit einer einzelnen Notiz. Das Dropdown-Item kГ¶nnte entsprechend "Alle Notizen nach Sheets" heiГҹen.

Meine Empfehlung: **2 Spalten, 1 Zeile** вҖ” Spalte A = Titel, Spalte B = Markdown-Content. Einfach, direkt nutzbar zum Weiterverarbeiten.

Ich verstehe вҖ” Sheets exportiert nur **Tabellen**, keinen Freitext. Die Fehlermeldung bestГӨtigt: NotebookLM scannt den Content nach Tabellen-Strukturen und exportiert deren Zeilen/Spalten in ein Sheet.

**Frage 3: Sollen wir Markdown-Tabellen parsen (GFM-Syntax `| col | col |`), oder auch HTML-Tabellen aus dem TipTap-Editor?**

Meine Empfehlung: **Nur Markdown-Tabellen** вҖ” der TipTap-Editor schreibt GFM-Tabellen, und `marked` (haben wir bereits) kann die parsen. HTML-Tabellen braucht der Editor gar nicht zu erzeugen.

5. Neuer Tab Г¶ffnet das fertige Spreadsheet

Gleiche OAuth-EinschrГӨnkung wie bei Docs вҖ” Google Cloud Console muss die Sheets API aktiviert haben und den Account als Testnutzer eingetragen.

## рҹ§‘ User

Base directory for this skill: /home/homior/.claude/skills/tdd

# Test-Driven Development

## Philosophy

**Core principle**: Tests should verify behavior through public interfaces, not implementation details. Code can change entirely; tests shouldn't.

**Good tests** are integration-style: they exercise real code paths through public APIs. They describe _what_ the system does, not _how_ it does it. A good test reads like a specification - "user can checkout with valid cart" tells you exactly what capability exists. These tests survive refactors because they don't care about internal structure.

**Bad tests** are coupled to implementation. They mock internal collaborators, test private methods, or verify through external means (like querying a database directly instead of using the interface). The warning sign: your test breaks when you refactor, but behavior hasn't changed. If you rename an internal function and tests fail, those tests were testing implementation, not behavior.

See [tests.md](tests.md) for examples and [mocking.md](mocking.md) for mocking guidelines.

## Anti-Pattern: Horizontal Slices

**DO NOT write all tests first, then all implementation.** This is "horizontal slicing" - treating RED as "write all tests" and GREEN as "write all code."

This produces **crap tests**:

- Tests written in bulk test _imagined_ behavior, not _actual_ behavior
- You end up testing the _shape_ of things (data structures, function signatures) rather than user-facing behavior
- Tests become insensitive to real changes - they pass when behavior breaks, fail when behavior is fine
- You outrun your headlights, committing to test structure before understanding the implementation

**Correct approach**: Vertical slices via tracer bullets. One test вҶ’ one implementation вҶ’ repeat. Each test responds to what you learned from the previous cycle. Because you just wrote the code, you know exactly what behavior matters and how to verify it.

```
WRONG (horizontal):
  RED:   test1, test2, test3, test4, test5
  GREEN: impl1, impl2, impl3, impl4, impl5

RIGHT (vertical):
  REDвҶ’GREEN: test1вҶ’impl1
  REDвҶ’GREEN: test2вҶ’impl2
  REDвҶ’GREEN: test3вҶ’impl3
  ...
```

## Workflow

### 1. Planning

When exploring the codebase, read `CONTEXT.md` (if it exists) so that test names and interface vocabulary match the project's domain language, and respect ADRs in the area you're touching.

Before writing any code:

- [ ] Confirm with user what interface changes are needed
- [ ] Confirm with user which behaviors to test (prioritize)
- [ ] Identify opportunities for deep modules (small interface, deep implementation) вҖ” run the `/codebase-design` skill for the vocabulary and the testability checks
- [ ] List the behaviors to test (not implementation steps)
- [ ] Get user approval on the plan

Ask: "What should the public interface look like? Which behaviors are most important to test?"

**You can't test everything.** Confirm with the user exactly which behaviors matter most. Focus testing effort on critical paths and complex logic, not every possible edge case.

### 2. Tracer Bullet

Write ONE test that confirms ONE thing about the system:

```
RED:   Write test for first behavior вҶ’ test fails
GREEN: Write minimal code to pass вҶ’ test passes
```

This is your tracer bullet - proves the path works end-to-end.

### 3. Incremental Loop

For each remaining behavior:

```
RED:   Write next test вҶ’ fails
GREEN: Minimal code to pass вҶ’ passes
```

Rules:

- One test at a time
- Only enough code to pass current test
- Don't anticipate future tests
- Keep tests focused on observable behavior

### 4. Refactor

After all tests pass, look for [refactor candidates](refactoring.md):

- [ ] Extract duplication
- [ ] Deepen modules (move complexity behind simple interfaces)
- [ ] Apply SOLID principles where natural
- [ ] Consider what new code reveals about existing code
- [ ] Run tests after each refactor step

**Never refactor while RED.** Get to GREEN first.

## Checklist Per Cycle

```
[ ] Test describes behavior, not implementation
[ ] Test uses public interface only
[ ] Test would survive internal refactor
[ ] Code is minimal for this test
[ ] No speculative features added
```

ARGUMENTS: Feature: Studio Quiz (Issue #14). Kontext: YannBookLM, ein Quiz-Tool das auf Basis der Quellen des Notebooks generiert wird. Issue-Beschreibung: "15 Multiple-Choice Fragen, A/B/C/D, Sofort-Feedback". Offene Fragen: Wie sieht der genaue Flow aus? Wird das Quiz einmalig generiert und gespeichert, oder bei jedem Klick neu generiert? Soll es eine Fortschrittsanzeige geben? Wie wird das Ergebnis am Ende dargestellt?

---

## рҹӨ– Assistant

**Frage 1: Wird das Quiz einmalig generiert und als StudioOutput gespeichert, oder bei jedem Klick neu generiert?**

Meine Empfehlung: **Einmalig generiert und gespeichert** вҖ” wie bei anderen Studio-Outputs, damit der User das Quiz spГӨter nochmal ansehen kann ohne Credits zu verbrauchen.

Meine Empfehlung: **Sofort generieren mit Lade-Spinner** вҖ” kein Dialog, direkt losgehen. Weniger Klicks, bessere UX. Г„hnlich wie bei anderen Studio-Tools.

## рҹӨ– Assistant

Ich habe alles. Hier die vollstГӨndige Zusammenfassung vor der Implementierung:

**Quiz-Design:**
- Klick auf "Quiz" im Grid вҶ’ immer neues Quiz generieren (neuer StudioOutput, Spinner)
- Klick auf Quiz in der Ergebnisliste вҶ’ bestehendes Quiz Г¶ffnen
- Eine Frage nach der anderen, A/B/C/D + "Гңberspringen"-Button
- Sofort-Feedback nach Antwort (grГјn/rot + ErklГӨrung + "Weiter")
- Header: Back + "Neu generieren" (neuer StudioOutput)
- Ergebnisscreen: Kreis, Richtig/Falsch/Гңbersprungen, Behandelte Themen, Weiterlernen mit Topic-Chips + "Quiz erstellen" (Гјberschreibt aktuellen StudioOutput)
- "Quiz noch einmal ansehen" вҶ’ Review-Modus (korrekte Antworten pre-highlighted)
- "Quiz wiederholen" вҶ’ Neustart frisch
- 50:50 Split mit Chat (wie Notiz-Editor)

**JSON-Struktur in `data`:**
```ts
{ questions: [{ question, options: string[], correct: number, explanation }][], topics: string[], suggestions: string[] }
```

Fertig. Zusammenfassung was gebaut wurde:

**API** (`/api/studio/quiz`): Claude `generateObject` mit Zod-Schema вҶ’ 15 Fragen + Topics + Suggestions als JSON in `studioOutputs.data`. Optional `focusTopic` + `outputId` zum Гңberschreiben.

**QuizView**: Frage-fГјr-Frage UI mit Fortschrittsbalken, A-D Buttons, "Гңberspringen", Sofort-Feedback (grГјn/rot + ErklГӨrung), Ergebnisscreen (SVG-Kreis, Statistiken, Behandelte Themen, Weiterlernen-Chips), Review-Modus mit vormarkierten Antworten.

**StudioSidebar**: Klick auf "Quiz" вҶ’ sofort generieren + Г¶ffnen; Klick auf Quiz in Ergebnisliste вҶ’ Г¶ffnen; "Neu generieren" вҶ’ neuer StudioOutput; "Quiz erstellen" aus Weiterlernen вҶ’ Гјberschreibt aktuellen Output.

## рҹӨ– Assistant

Das ist kein Standard-Google-API вҖ” das ist NotebookLMs eigenes Share-UI. Es erfordert:

- Ein eigenes Berechtigungsmodell (wer darf das Notebook sehen)
- E-Mail-Einladungen
- Г–ffentliche/eingeschrГӨnkte Link-Optionen
- Access-Control-Middleware fГјr freigegebene Inhalte

Das ist ein eigenes Feature-Paket, nicht ein einzelner Button.

**Frage 2: Soll "Teilen" fГјr diese Demo ein vollwertiges Permission-System (wie im Screenshot) bekommen, oder reicht erstmal "Link kopieren" вҖ” die aktuelle Notebook-URL in die Zwischenablage?**

Meine Empfehlung: **Link kopieren** fГјr jetzt вҖ” die URL des Notebooks in die Zwischenablage, Toast "Link kopiert". Das Permission-System wГӨre ein eigenes Issue.

## рҹӨ– Assistant

Ergebnis: Das NotebookLM-Share-Dialog ist **komplett custom gebaut** вҖ” kein Google-Standard. Es gibt kein einbettbares Widget das das abdeckt.

Optionen fГјr uns:
1. **Web Share API** (`navigator.share`) вҖ” zeigt das native OS-Share-Sheet (funktioniert auf Chrome/Safari, v.a. auf Mobile), aber nicht customizable
2. **"Link kopieren"** вҖ” URL in die Zwischenablage, fertig
3. **VollstГӨndiges Permission-System** вҖ” eigenes Issue, erheblicher Aufwand

**Was mГ¶chtest du?**

## рҹ§‘ User

Base directory for this skill: /home/homior/.claude/skills/dev-server

# Dev Server вҖ” Start & Monitor

Start the Next.js dev server and stream errors from its log file.

## Steps

### 1. Kill any existing server on port 3000

```bash
fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1
```

### 2. Start the server using Bash run_in_background

Use the Bash tool with `run_in_background: true`. Save the background task ID вҖ” the output file path is needed for the Monitor in step 4.

Load the correct Node version via nvm before starting:

```
Bash(
  command:           "fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true; sleep 1; source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use && npm run dev",
  run_in_background: true
)
```

The tool returns a task ID and output path like:
`/tmp/claude-1000/.../tasks/<id>.output`

Save this path вҖ” it is used in step 4.

### 3. Wait until the server responds

```bash
until curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000 | grep -qE "200|302|307"; do sleep 1; done && echo "Server ready"
```

### 4. Start the persistent Monitor

Filter the log for errors and warnings only вҖ” do not emit every line.

```
Monitor(
  command:     "tail -f <output-path-from-step-2> 2>&1 | grep -E --line-buffered -i 'error|warn|failed|unhandled|exception|TypeError|ReferenceError|SyntaxError|cannot'",
  description: "Next.js dev server errors and warnings",
  persistent:  true
)
```

## On error notification

When the Monitor fires:

1. Read the last 60 lines of the log for full context:
   ```bash
   tail -60 .next/dev/logs/next-development.log
   ```
2. Identify root cause (missing table вҶ’ DrizzleAdapter; JWEInvalid вҶ’ session strategy mismatch; 42P01 вҶ’ wrong table name, etc.)
3. Fix the code, verify with `npm run typecheck && npm run lint`
4. Inform the user of what was fixed.

## Important rules

- **Never print .env values** вҖ” only check key existence: `grep -q "KEY" .env && echo set || echo missing`
- Do not use `&` in Bash tool calls вҖ” use `run_in_background: true` or the Node spawn approach above
- `nohup ... &` and `... &` both fail with exit code 144 in this environment вҖ” always use the Node spawn detach pattern

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

**Issue #9 (Notes) completion tasks:**
- 50:50 layout: note editor and chat should share space equally, not hide chat
- Empty text source validation: content must have вүҘ 5 chars to enable "Als Quelle festlegen"
- Character count display: `0/5` (red below min), `n/10.000` (gray above min)
- Note dropdown menu: icons + whitespace-nowrap + w-max + correct positioning
- Source sidebar dropdown: same w-max + whitespace-nowrap
- E2E tests: create note, delete note, set as source + delete source
- Issue #9 closure (chat export + GitHub close)

**Issue #14 (Quiz):**
- Grilling session before implementation
- Einmalig generiert und gespeichert as StudioOutput
- Clicking "Quiz" in tools grid ALWAYS generates new quiz (new StudioOutput)
- One question at a time, A/B/C/D + "Гңberspringen" button (direct skip without answering)
- Sofort-Feedback per answer (green/red + explanation)
- Ergebnisscreen: circle progress (SVG), Richtig/Falsch/Гңbersprungen stats, Behandelte Themen, Weiterlernen topic chips + "Quiz erstellen" (overwrites current output), "Quiz noch einmal ansehen" (review mode), "Quiz wiederholen" (fresh restart)
- Review mode: correct answers pre-highlighted with "Richtige Antwort(Гјbersprungen)" label
- StudioSidebar (not fullscreen), 50:50 split with chat
- JSON stored in `studioOutputs.data`: `{ questions: [{question, options: string[], correct: number, explanation}][], topics: string[], suggestions: string[] }`
- Context-aware dropdown: quiz items вҶ’ Teilen/Umbenennen/Prompt ansehen/LГ¶schen; note items вҶ’ existing menu

**Rename feature (current):**
- "Umbenennen" from quiz dropdown вҶ’ small inline dialog in list entry
- `title` column added to `studioOutputs` table
- PATCH endpoint to update title
- `studioOutputToItem` uses `o.title ?? tStudio(o.type)` as fallback

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- Next.js App Router with Server Components + Client Components
- Drizzle ORM with `drizzle-kit push` for schema migrations
- `generateObject` from Vercel AI SDK with Zod schema for structured Claude output
- `pgEnum` for `studioOutputTypeEnum` (audio, mindmap, slidedeck, datatable, quiz)
- `jsonb` column for storing quiz data
- Playwright E2E tests with `fixtures.local.ts` (JWT cookie injection)
- `next-intl` for i18n (useTranslations, useLocale)
- Radix UI Dialog, DropdownMenu components (shadcn/ui)
- TipTap editor (ProseMirror) for note content
- GIS (Google Identity Services) for OAuth popup (drive.file, spreadsheets scopes)
- SVG circle progress indicator (manual calculation)
- React state machine for quiz (quiz/review/results modes)

3. Files and Code Sections:

- **`components/notebook/notebook-shell.tsx`**
  - Changed `<main>` from `noteMode ? "w-0" : "flex-1"` to always `flex-1`
  - Removed unused `cn` import
  ```tsx
  <main className="flex flex-1 overflow-hidden">
  ```

- **`components/notebook/studio-sidebar.tsx`** (major changes throughout session)
  - Added `studioOutputsList` state (was using `initialStudioOutputs` directly)
  - Added `activeQuiz`, `quizLoading`, `renamingOutput`, `renameValue` state
  - Updated `studioOutputToItem` to use `o.title ?? tStudio(o.type)`
  - Added `openQuiz()`, `closeQuiz()`, `generateQuiz()`, `handleRenameStudioOutput()`, `handleDeleteStudioOutput()`
  - Context-aware dropdown: quiz items get Teilen/Umbenennen/Prompt/LГ¶schen; note items keep existing menu
  - Quiz view renders in sidebar like note detail (same 50:50 split via `onNoteModeChange(true)`)
  - Rename dialog added at end of return wrapped in `<>...</>`
  - Character count in note footer: `{belowMin ? `${len}/5` : `${len.toLocaleString()}/10.000`}`
  - Trash button in note title bar: `aria-label={t("deleteNote")}`
  - Important: `studioOutputToItem` fallback: `o.title ?? tStudio(o.type)`

- **`components/notebook/quiz-view.tsx`** (NEW)
  - Full quiz UI: question-by-question, progress bar, options (A-D), skip button, feedback, results screen
  - `Mode = "quiz" | "review" | "results"`
  - `AnswerState = { selected: number | null; skipped: boolean }`
  - `CircleProgress` component with SVG
  - Review mode: `getOptionClass()` shows correct answer in green, others dimmed
  - Results screen: circle + stats + Behandelte Themen + Weiterlernen chips + "Quiz erstellen" button
  - Props: `data: QuizData, outputId: string, onNewQuizFromTopic: (topic, outputId) => void`

- **`app/api/studio/quiz/route.ts`** (NEW)
  - POST endpoint with `generateObject` + Zod schema (15 questions, topics, suggestions)
  - Accepts optional `focusTopic` and `outputId` (to overwrite existing)
  - `getSourceContent()`: fetches up to 60 parent chunks from ready+enabled sources
  - Returns new or updated StudioOutput

- **`app/api/studio/quiz/[id]/route.ts`** (NEW)
  - PATCH: updates `title` field on studioOutput (rename)
  - DELETE: removes studioOutput (with ownership check via notebooks table)

- **`db/schema.ts`**
  - Added `title: text("title")` to `studioOutputs` table (nullable)
  - Migration applied via `drizzle-kit push`

- **`messages/de.json`** and **`messages/en.json`**
  - Added under `studio`: `quizRegenerate`, `quizGenerating`, `quizNoSources`, `quizError`, `share`, `rename`, `viewPrompt`, `delete`
  - **MISSING** (causing current errors): `studio.cancel`, `studio.save`, `studio.renameError`

- **`tests/notes.local.spec.ts`** (NEW)
  - 3 tests using `fixtures.local.ts`, all passing in 22s
  - Helpers: `createNotebook()`, `tryDeleteNotebook()`, `studioSidebar = page.locator("aside").last()`, `sourceSidebar = page.locator("aside").first()`
  - Test 1: Click FAB вҶ’ expect title input visible
  - Test 2: Click FAB вҶ’ click Trash button (aria-label="LГ¶schen") вҶ’ expect "Noch keine Ergebnisse"
  - Test 3: Create note, type content in `.ProseMirror`, wait 2.5s, click "Als Quelle festlegen", verify source in left aside, delete source

- **`lib/google-docs-export.ts`**
  - `exportNoteToGoogleDocs()`: GIS popup (drive.file scope) вҶ’ marked MDвҶ’HTML вҶ’ Drive multipart upload
  - `exportNoteToGoogleSheets()`: GIS popup (spreadsheets scope) вҶ’ parse MD tables вҶ’ Sheets API
  - `parseMarkdownTables()`: GFM table parser вҶ’ `string[][][]`
  - `getAccessToken(clientId, scope)`: refactored to accept scope parameter

4. Errors and Fixes:

- **`createdAt.getTime is not a function`**: API JSON response has `createdAt` as string, not Date. Fixed by casting and converting: `const output = { ...raw, createdAt: new Date(raw.createdAt) }`

- **`Bookmarks` not exported from lucide-react**: Replaced with `Library` icon

- **`studio_DUPLICATE_REMOVE` accidentally created**: When adding studio keys, accidentally created a duplicate namespace entry. Fixed by reading the file and removing the spurious block.

- **JSX must have one parent element**: `return (<aside>...</aside><Dialog>...)` вҖ” fixed by wrapping in `<>...</>`

- **MISSING_MESSAGE studio.cancel / studio.save**: Not yet fixed вҖ” this is the current error. Needs to be added to both `messages/de.json` and `messages/en.json` under `studio` namespace. Also `studio.renameError` is referenced in code.

- **`Bookmarks` icon**: doesn't exist in lucide-react вҶ’ replaced with `Library`

- **`w-0` hiding chat**: Changed to always `flex-1` so note editor shares space 50:50

- **JSON parse error in de.json**: Previous session issue with unicode quotes in i18n values

- **Background task hung**: First `npx playwright test` background run got stuck; ran synchronously instead вҶ’ 3/3 passed

5. Problem Solving:

- **50:50 split**: Both `<main>` and StudioSidebar get `flex-1` when noteMode is true вҖ” achieved by removing the conditional `w-0` class from `<main>`
- **Quiz state machine**: Three modes (quiz/review/results) managed in `QuizView` component; answers tracked as `AnswerState[]` array indexed by question number
- **Multiple quizzes**: `studioOutputsList` state (not `initialStudioOutputs`) allows dynamic additions without page reload
- **"Quiz erstellen" from Weiterlernen**: Calls `onNewQuizFromTopic(topic, outputId)` which calls `generateQuiz(topic, outputId)` вҶ’ PATCH overwrites current StudioOutput's data
- **Rename UX**: Small inline Dialog (not full modal) with Input + Enter key support + Save/Cancel buttons

Base directory for this skill: /home/homior/.claude/skills/tdd

# Test-Driven Development

## Philosophy

**Core principle**: Tests should verify behavior through public interfaces, not implementation details. Code can change entirely; tests shouldn't.

**Good tests** are integration-style: they exercise real code paths through public APIs. They describe _what_ the system does, not _how_ it does it. A good test reads like a specification - "user can checkout with valid cart" tells you exactly what capability exists. These tests survive refactors because they don't care about internal structure.

**Bad tests** are coupled to implementation. They mock internal collaborators, test private methods, or verify through external means (like querying a database directly instead of using the interface). The warning sign: your test breaks when you refactor, but behavior hasn't changed. If you rename an internal function and tests fail, those tests were testing implementation, not behavior.

See [tests.md](tests.md) for examples and [mocking.md](mocking.md) for mocking guidelines.

## Anti-Pattern: Horizontal Slices

**DO NOT write all tests first, then all implementation.** This is "horizontal slicing" - treating RED as "write all tests" and GREEN as "write all code."

This produces **crap tests**:

- Tests written in bulk test _imagined_ behavior, not _actual_ behavior
- You end up testing the _shape_ of things (data structures, function signatures) rather than user-facing behavior
- Tests become insensitive to real changes - they pass when behavior breaks, fail when behavior is fine
- You outrun your headlights, committing to test structure before understanding the implementation

**Correct approach**: Vertical slices via tracer bullets. One test вҶ’ one implementation вҶ’ repeat. Each test responds to what you learned from the previous cycle. Because you just wrote the code, you know exactly what behavior matters and how to verify it.

```
WRONG (horizontal):
  RED:   test1, test2, test3, test4, test5
  GREEN: impl1, impl2, impl3, impl4, impl5

RIGHT (vertical):
  REDвҶ’GREEN: test1вҶ’impl1
  REDвҶ’GREEN: test2вҶ’impl2
  REDвҶ’GREEN: test3вҶ’impl3
  ...
```

## Workflow

### 1. Planning

When exploring the codebase, read `CONTEXT.md` (if it exists) so that test names and interface vocabulary match the project's domain language, and respect ADRs in the area you're touching.

Before writing any code:

- [ ] Confirm with user what interface changes are needed
- [ ] Confirm with user which behaviors to test (prioritize)
- [ ] Identify opportunities for deep modules (small interface, deep implementation) вҖ” run the `/codebase-design` skill for the vocabulary and the testability checks
- [ ] List the behaviors to test (not implementation steps)
- [ ] Get user approval on the plan

Ask: "What should the public interface look like? Which behaviors are most important to test?"

**You can't test everything.** Confirm with the user exactly which behaviors matter most. Focus testing effort on critical paths and complex logic, not every possible edge case.

### 2. Tracer Bullet

Write ONE test that confirms ONE thing about the system:

```
RED:   Write test for first behavior вҶ’ test fails
GREEN: Write minimal code to pass вҶ’ test passes
```

This is your tracer bullet - proves the path works end-to-end.

### 3. Incremental Loop

For each remaining behavior:

```
RED:   Write next test вҶ’ fails
GREEN: Minimal code to pass вҶ’ passes
```

Rules:

- One test at a time
- Only enough code to pass current test
- Don't anticipate future tests
- Keep tests focused on observable behavior

### 4. Refactor

After all tests pass, look for [refactor candidates](refactoring.md):

- [ ] Extract duplication
- [ ] Deepen modules (move complexity behind simple interfaces)
- [ ] Apply SOLID principles where natural
- [ ] Consider what new code reveals about existing code
- [ ] Run tests after each refactor step

**Never refactor while RED.** Get to GREEN first.

## Checklist Per Cycle

```
[ ] Test describes behavior, not implementation
[ ] Test uses public interface only
[ ] Test would survive internal refactor
[ ] Code is minimal for this test
[ ] No speculative features added
```


ARGUMENTS: Playwright E2E tests fГјr Quiz-Feature: Quiz erstellen (Klick auf Quiz-Button im Tools-Grid вҶ’ Spinner erscheint in Ergebnisliste), Quiz umbenennen (Drei-Punkte-MenГј вҶ’ Umbenennen вҶ’ Inline-Dialog вҶ’ Speichern), Quiz lГ¶schen (Drei-Punkte-MenГј вҶ’ LГ¶schen), Modal "Prompt und Quellen ansehen" Г¶ffnen (Quellen-Chips sichtbar), Klick auf вҶә вҶ’ Anpassen-Ansicht im Modal sichtbar, dann gemockter Klick auf "Generieren". Bestehende Playwright-Infrastruktur mit JWT-Session-Fixtures vorhanden (fixtures.local.ts). Vorhandene Tests in tests/notes.local.spec.ts als Referenz nutzen. WICHTIG: Quiz-Generierung ist API-abhГӨngig (Claude API) вҖ” der "Erstellen"-Test soll nur prГјfen ob der Spinner erscheint, nicht das fertige Quiz. Den Generieren-Klick im Modal per Route-Mock abfangen.

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   **Issue #14 (Quiz) completion tasks** вҖ” Started the session fixing missing i18n keys (`studio.cancel`, `studio.save`) by switching to `tCommon`. Then implemented a series of features:
   - **Share (Teilen)**: Magic link via UUID token stored in `studioOutputs.share_token`, public route `/share/[token]` (no auth), Web Share API with clipboard fallback
   - **Public quiz adjustments**: `readOnly` prop on QuizView hides "Quiz erstellen" + Weiterlernen section; stats no longer reset on "Quiz noch einmal ansehen"; full-width centered layout on share page; circular SVG progress ring on question screen; fixed SVG ring visibility using Tailwind `stroke-*` classes
   - **Quiz Sources Modal**: "Prompt und Quellen ansehen" opens a two-view modal (sources chips / customize view with count 10/15/25, difficulty einfach/mittel/schwierig, focusTopic textarea). Modal uses `sm:max-w-2xl`, buttons are `items-start` auto-width. вҶә button at `absolute right-12 top-4`
   - **Quiz API**: Stores `usedSources: { id, title, type }[]` in data JSON; accepts `count` and `difficulty` params; `difficultyContext` map sends narrative per selected level only
   - **Spinner in results list**: Loading placeholder (Loader2) for new quizzes; `regeneratingOutputId` state for regenerating existing ones
   - **Source chip click**: Closes modal, opens source detail panel in left sidebar via custom DOM event `yannbooklm:open-source`
   - **E2E Tests**: `tests/quiz.local.spec.ts` with 5 tests covering all above flows using mocked API routes

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Next.js App Router with middleware for auth bypass (`isSharePage` early return)
   - Drizzle ORM + `drizzle-kit push` (migration via raw SQL when TTY unavailable)
   - `generateObject` from Vercel AI SDK with dynamic Zod schema (`questions.length(count)`)
   - `crypto.randomUUID()` for share tokens
   - Web Share API (`navigator.share`) with clipboard fallback
   - Radix UI Dialog, DropdownMenu (shadcn/ui); `data-slot="dropdown-menu-trigger"`
   - SVG circle progress (manual dasharray calculation); Tailwind `stroke-*` utility classes
   - Custom DOM events (`yannbooklm:open-source`) for cross-component communication
   - `useRef` pattern for calling functions defined after `useEffect`
   - Playwright `page.route()` for API mocking in E2E tests

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`components/notebook/studio-sidebar.tsx`**
     - Added `tCommon = useTranslations("common")` for cancel/save
     - Added `newQuizLoading`, `regeneratingOutputId`, `sourcesModalOutput` states
     - `generateQuiz(focusTopic?, outputId?, count?, difficulty?)` вҖ” passes new params
     - Loading placeholder in `outputs` array: `{ id: "__loading__", kind: "quiz", loading: true }`
     - Spinner icon `Loader2` when `item.loading || item.id === regeneratingOutputId`
     - DropdownMenu wrapped in `{!item.loading && (...)}` 
     - "Prompt und Quellen ansehen" wired to `setSourcesModalOutput(studioOutputsList.find(o => o.id === item.id))`
     - `<QuizSourcesModal>` added at end of return
     - Share button calls `POST /api/studio/quiz/[id]/share` вҶ’ gets token вҶ’ `navigator.share` or clipboard
   
   - **`components/notebook/quiz-view.tsx`**
     - Added `QuizUsedSource` type: `{ id: string; title: string; type: string }`
     - Extended `QuizData`: `usedSources?: QuizUsedSource[]`
     - Added `readOnly?: boolean` prop
     - `QuizCircleProgress` component replacing linear bar on question screen (size-16, r=28)
     - `CircleProgress`: stroke uses `className="stroke-muted-foreground/25"` and `className="stroke-primary"`
     - `restart(reviewMode)`: only resets answers when `!reviewMode`
     - Results screen: `cn(..., readOnly && "items-center")` on outer div
     - Weiterlernen section: wrapped in `{!readOnly && (...)}`
     - Grid: `readOnly ? "flex flex-col gap-4" : "grid grid-cols-2 gap-4"`
     - Buttons row: `cn("flex gap-2 border-t pt-4", readOnly && "justify-center")`

   - **`components/notebook/quiz-sources-modal.tsx`** (NEW)
     ```tsx
     type View = "sources" | "customize"
     type Difficulty = "einfach" | "mittel" | "schwierig"
     
     // Source icons by type
     const SOURCE_ICONS: Record<string, React.ReactNode> = {
       pdf: <FileText .../>,
       url: <Globe .../>,
       youtube: <FaYoutube .../>,
       text: <AlignLeft />,
     }
     
     const COUNT_OPTIONS = [
       { label: "Weniger", value: 10 },
       { label: "Standard (Standardeinstellung)", value: 15 },
       { label: "Mehr", value: 25 },
     ]
     const DIFFICULTY_OPTIONS = [
       { label: "Einfach", value: "einfach" },
       { label: "Mittel (Standardeinstellung)", value: "mittel" },
       { label: "Schwierig", value: "schwierig" },
     ]
     
     // вҶә button: absolute right-12 top-4 to avoid overlapping Dialog X
     // Sources: clickable buttons вҶ’ onOpenChange(false); openSourceById(s.id)
     // Customize: items-start auto-width buttons
     // Generieren: onGenerate({ count, difficulty, focusTopic }) вҶ’ closes modal
     ```

   - **`app/api/studio/quiz/route.ts`**
     - `type Difficulty = "einfach" | "mittel" | "schwierig"`
     - `difficultyContext: Record<Difficulty, string>` вҖ” narrative per level
     - `getSourceContent()` returns `{ content, usedSources: { id, title, type }[] }`
     - Dynamic schema: `z.array(...).length(count)`
     - Prompt: `Schwierigkeitsgrad: ${difficulty} вҖ“ ${difficultyContext[difficulty]}`
     - Stores `{ ...object, usedSources }` in data

   - **`app/api/studio/quiz/[id]/share/route.ts`** (NEW)
     - POST: generates/returns UUID share token (idempotent, checks ownership via notebooks join)

   - **`app/api/studio/quiz/[id]/route.ts`**
     - PATCH: updates `title`; DELETE: removes output (with ownership check)

   - **`app/share/[token]/page.tsx`** (NEW)
     - Public page, no auth, loads quiz by shareToken, renders SharedQuizClient
     - `max-w-4xl mx-auto px-6 py-6`

   - **`app/share/[token]/shared-quiz-client.tsx`** (NEW)
     - `"use client"` wrapper: `<QuizView data={data} outputId={outputId} onNewQuizFromTopic={() => undefined} readOnly />`

   - **`middleware.ts`**
     - Added `const isSharePage = pathname.startsWith("/share/")`
     - Early return: `if (isSharePage) return NextResponse.next()`

   - **`db/schema.ts`**
     - Added `shareToken: text("share_token").unique()` to studioOutputs
     - Migration via raw SQL: `ALTER TABLE studio_outputs ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS share_token TEXT UNIQUE;`

   - **`lib/source-view-event.ts`**
     - Added `openSourceById(sourceId: string)` dispatching `yannbooklm:open-source` event
     - Added `onOpenSourceById(cb)` listener

   - **`components/notebook/source-sidebar.tsx`**
     - Added `handleSourceClickRef = useRef<...>()` 
     - `useEffect` with `onOpenSourceById` вҶ’ finds source by ID вҶ’ calls `handleSourceClickRef.current(source)`
     - `useEffect(() => { handleSourceClickRef.current = handleSourceClick })` to keep ref current
     - Source list items: added `id={`source-${source.id}`}`

   - **`messages/de.json`** and **`messages/en.json`**
     - Added `studio.renameError`, `studio.shareCopied`, `studio.shareError`

   - **`tests/quiz.local.spec.ts`** (NEW вҖ” currently running)
     ```ts
     const MOCK_QUIZ_ID = "mock-quiz-e2e-abc123"
     const MOCK_QUIZ_OUTPUT = { id: MOCK_QUIZ_ID, type: "quiz", data: { questions: [...], usedSources: [{ id: "src-001", title: "Mathe-Buch.pdf", type: "pdf" }] } }
     
     // 5 tests:
     // 1. Spinner: mock with 4s delay, click Quiz button, expect animate-spin
     // 2. Rename: mock POST + PATCH, createMockQuizAndWait, hover вҶ’ dropdown-trigger вҶ’ Umbenennen вҶ’ fill вҶ’ Speichern вҶ’ check "Mein Quiz"
     // 3. Delete: mock POST + DELETE 204, dropdown вҶ’ LГ¶schen вҶ’ expect "Noch keine Ergebnisse"
     // 4. Modal Quellen: mock POST, open dropdown вҶ’ "Prompt und Quellen ansehen" вҶ’ expect "Mathe-Buch.pdf"
     // 5. Anpassen: open modal вҶ’ click "Quiz anpassen" вҶ’ check "Anzahl der Fragen" visible вҶ’ mock POST вҶ’ click "Generieren" вҶ’ expect dialog gone
     ```

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **`studio.cancel`/`studio.save` MISSING_MESSAGE**: Fixed by adding `tCommon = useTranslations("common")` and using `tCommon("cancel")` / `tCommon("save")`
   - **`drizzle-kit push` needs TTY** for unique constraint prompt: Fixed by running raw SQL `ALTER TABLE studio_outputs ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS share_token TEXT UNIQUE;`
   - **`Textarea` not in shadcn/ui**: Used native `<textarea>` with Tailwind classes
   - **`Youtube` not in lucide-react**: Used `FaYoutube` from `react-icons/fa`
   - **`output` not in scope** in dropdown click handler: Used `studioOutputsList.find((o) => o.id === item.id)`
   - **JSX parsing error** `{!item.loading && <DropdownMenu>...</DropdownMenu>}`: Fixed by wrapping closing tag correctly with `{!item.loading && (<DropdownMenu>...</DropdownMenu>)}`
   - **SVG ring invisible** on dark background: `hsl(var(--muted))` too dark вҶ’ switched to Tailwind `stroke-muted-foreground/25` and `stroke-primary`
   - **`handleSourceClickRef.current = handleSourceClick` during render**: Fixed by wrapping in `useEffect(() => { handleSourceClickRef.current = handleSourceClick })`
   - **`[cannot access refs during render]`**: Same as above

5. Problem Solving:
   - Share token idempotency: `if (output.shareToken) return NextResponse.json({ token: output.shareToken })` before generating new one
   - Dynamic Zod schema for variable question count: moved `QuizSchema` inside `POST` handler where `count` is available
   - Buttons in customize modal too wide: changed to `flex flex-col items-start gap-2` instead of `flex flex-col gap-2`
   - вҶә button overlapping Dialog's X: positioned `absolute right-12 top-4` to be left of the Dialog's own X at `right-4 top-4`
   - Cross-component navigation (quiz modal вҶ’ source sidebar): custom DOM event `yannbooklm:open-source` dispatched from modal, listened in source sidebar

## рҹ§‘ User

Base directory for this skill: /home/homior/.claude/skills/dev-server

# Dev Server вҖ” Start & Monitor

Start the Next.js dev server and stream errors from its log file.

## Steps

### 1. Kill any existing server on port 3000

```bash
fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1
```

### 2. Start the server using Bash run_in_background

Use the Bash tool with `run_in_background: true`. Save the background task ID вҖ” the output file path is needed for the Monitor in step 4.

Load the correct Node version via nvm before starting:

```
Bash(
  command:           "fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true; sleep 1; source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use && npm run dev",
  run_in_background: true
)
```

The tool returns a task ID and output path like:
`/tmp/claude-1000/.../tasks/<id>.output`

Save this path вҖ” it is used in step 4.

### 3. Wait until the server responds

```bash
until curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000 | grep -qE "200|302|307"; do sleep 1; done && echo "Server ready"
```

### 4. Start the persistent Monitor

Filter the log for errors and warnings only вҖ” do not emit every line.

```
Monitor(
  command:     "tail -f <output-path-from-step-2> 2>&1 | grep -E --line-buffered -i 'error|warn|failed|unhandled|exception|TypeError|ReferenceError|SyntaxError|cannot'",
  description: "Next.js dev server errors and warnings",
  persistent:  true
)
```

## On error notification

When the Monitor fires:

1. Read the last 60 lines of the log for full context:
   ```bash
   tail -60 .next/dev/logs/next-development.log
   ```
2. Identify root cause (missing table вҶ’ DrizzleAdapter; JWEInvalid вҶ’ session strategy mismatch; 42P01 вҶ’ wrong table name, etc.)
3. Fix the code, verify with `npm run typecheck && npm run lint`
4. Inform the user of what was fixed.

## Important rules

- **Never print .env values** вҖ” only check key existence: `grep -q "KEY" .env && echo set || echo missing`
- Do not use `&` in Bash tool calls вҖ” use `run_in_background: true` or the Node spawn approach above
- `nohup ... &` and `... &` both fail with exit code 144 in this environment вҖ” always use the Node spawn detach pattern

Der Skill ist unter `~/.claude/skills/dev-agent/SKILL.md` installiert und sofort verfГјgbar.

**Verwendung:**
```
/dev-agent backend /tmp/tasks.log

**Ablauf:**
1. Startet einen persistenten Monitor auf die Log-Datei, gefiltert auf `[Task] Agent backend: `
2. Wenn eine Zeile matcht, extrahiert er die Task-Beschreibung
3. Startet eine Grilling-Session (eine Frage nach der anderen) um die Anforderungen zu klГӨren
4. Implementiert die LГ¶sung nach abgeschlossenem Grilling
5. Гңberwacht weiter auf den nГӨchsten Task

Das Log-Format muss dabei `[Task] Agent <name>: <beschreibung>` sein.
